root_cause: VERIFICATION_GAP
details: Two acceptance criteria unmet: (1) _persist_desktop_speaker_sidecar hardcodes name=None instead of using recognized name from enrollment, (2) no _fire_processing_progress call for voice enrollment step
fix: Return speaker_names from _enroll_desktop_voices, pass to sidecar; add progress broadcast before enrollment
prevention: PLAN should specify data flow between sequential processing steps; MAP-PLAN acceptance criteria should include explicit verification of data handoff between methods

root_cause: LLM_OUTPUT_SCHEMA
details: _apply_prompt_change_inner used change_detail.get('text') but Claude's analysis produced change_detail with 'add_line' key instead. Fallback to str(change_detail) printed raw dict into learned_behaviors.md.
fix: Changed to: change_detail.get('text') or change_detail.get('add_line') or str(change_detail)
prevention: When consuming LLM-generated JSON, handle multiple plausible keys. Or constrain the output schema in the analysis prompt.

root_cause: SERVER_DEP_MANAGEMENT
details: Used 'uv sync' on server to install jellyfish, which reconciled venv against lockfile and removed manually-installed packages (google-api-python-client, etc). Server uses 'uv run' via systemd but deps were installed with pip. Caused 502 Bad Gateway from ModuleNotFoundError: googleapiclient.
fix: Used 'uv pip install' instead of 'uv sync'. Reinstalled missing packages. Fixed with 'uv pip install -e .' and individual package installs.
prevention: NEVER use 'uv sync' on the server. Always use 'uv pip install <package>' for adding new dependencies. Server dep management is pip-based, not lockfile-based.

root_cause: BLOCKING_FIRE_AND_FORGET
details: web_ws.py was using 'await extract_and_enqueue()' which blocked the user response until extraction completed (~4.9s). Should be fire-and-forget since extraction results aren't needed for the response.
fix: Changed to asyncio.create_task(extract_and_enqueue(...)) for non-blocking background execution
prevention: Background tasks that don't affect the response should use asyncio.create_task(), not await. Review all callers of slow background operations.

root_cause: ASYNCPG_POOL_VS_CONNECTION
details: AuditLogger.record() called 'async with db.transaction():' where db is an asyncpg Pool returned by pool.get_pool(). Pool objects don't have .transaction() — that method exists on Connection objects. Caused AttributeError at runtime; events buffered in memory but never persisted.
fix: Changed to 'async with db.acquire() as conn: async with conn.transaction():' and updated all queries inside the block to use conn instead of db.
prevention: When writing DB code that requires transactions in this codebase, remember: get_pool() returns a Pool, not a Connection. Transactions require pool.acquire() first. Always verify object type (Pool vs Connection) before calling transaction().

root_cause: PIPECAT_PIPELINE_DEADLOCK
details: state_query broadcast goes to web WS client. Browser desktop.js handler receives it and sends state_response back. Response enters Pipecat's FastAPIWebsocketTransport recv loop, deserialized to InputTransportMessageFrame, needs to flow through pipeline to TextMessageRouter. But the pipeline is blocked — the tool handler is awaiting a Future for the response, and that same pipeline must process the incoming frame. Classic async deadlock with 2s timeout. Architecture: web.html loads web.js (patches /ws/desktop -> /ws/web) then desktop.js (connects to patched URL). Single WebSocket shared between Pipecat audio transport and chat/control messages via WebHybridSerializer.
fix: Replaced WebSocket round-trip with server-side state collection. Server already knows channel, execution path, client counts, operating mode, and voice status.
prevention: Never await a response from the same WebSocket pipeline that your handler is blocking. Pipecat's transport serializes frame processing — if a tool handler blocks, no new frames are processed. For browser state queries, either use a separate communication channel (HTTP endpoint, separate WS) or collect state server-side.

description: If source_id filter is required, plan doesn't include test validating plans without source_id are excluded
impact: Critical filter behavior not tested, regression risk
root_cause: Agent planned tests for explicit spec cases but missed implicit filter validation
lesson: Test ALL decision points in implementation logic, including filters

description: Plan states issue #156 resolved but doesn't explicitly confirm PR #167 added after_tax_contributions to cashflow_summary DataFrame (not just top-level output)
impact: Assumption not verified - implementation depends on specific location of data
root_cause: Agent stated dependency resolved but didn't verify exact implementation details
lesson: Verify exact location of dependencies (file, method, line numbers); don't assume implementation details

description: Empty/error state handling logic is correct but lacks explicit priority order documentation - lines 558 and 566 both return EmptyStateLayout, appears duplicated
impact: Code clarity issue, not runtime bug - unclear which state takes precedence
root_cause: Agent wrote functionally correct logic but didn't document state handling priority explicitly
lesson: Document state render priority order explicitly with comments; consolidate duplicate returns for clarity

root_cause: MISSING_TEST
details: No tests created for Scenario model or repository despite acceptance criteria explicitly requiring: Model and repository tests pass, pytest tests/scenarios_tests/ -q passes
fix: Create tests/scenarios_tests/ with test_models.py and test_repository.py
prevention: Read acceptance criteria literally - if tests required in phase, create tests in that phase

root_cause: LINT_ERROR
details: 5 unused imports in test file (AssetAuditDetail, AuditMetadata, DebtAuditDetail, ExpenseAuditDetail, IncomeAuditDetail)
prevention: Run ruff check on all changed files before committing

lesson: Never merge components with console.log stub handlers. Add integration tests for CRUD operations. Verify spec requirements against implementation.

root_cause: PATH_EXPANSION
details: Path(buddy_home) without .expanduser() caused YAML/prompt files to be written to literal ~/buddy directory inside project root instead of /Users/jasonjob/buddy. DB marked proposals as 'applied' but no actual file change occurred.
fix: Added .expanduser() to all 4 Path(buddy_home) calls in _apply_config_change_inner, _apply_prompt_change_inner, and rollback_proposal
prevention: Always use Path(...).expanduser() when path comes from config. PROVE should verify file exists at expected absolute path after write operations.

root_cause: SQL_RESERVED_WORD
details: 'symmetric' is a PostgreSQL reserved word. Used unquoted in CREATE TABLE and INSERT statements in init-db.sql, phase1 migration, and fact_vocabulary.py seed function. PostgreSQL rejected the DDL.
fix: Quoted as "symmetric" in all DDL and DML statements across 3 files
prevention: Always check column names against PostgreSQL reserved word list. Quote identifiers that match reserved words.

root_cause: WRONG_TABLE_NAME
details: Spec referenced 'grid_entity' table but actual table is 'entity'. _load_entity_index_with_context() queried non-existent table, causing extraction to fail silently.
fix: Changed FROM grid_entity to FROM entity in the SQL query
prevention: Always verify table names by reading init-db.sql or running \dt before writing queries. Never trust spec table names without verification.

root_cause: INVALID_SQL_CONSTRUCT
details: Used array_agg(DISTINCT fact_key ORDER BY confidence DESC) which is invalid PostgreSQL — cannot ORDER BY a column not in the DISTINCT list.
fix: Rewrote as correlated subquery with LIMIT 2 instead of aggregate
prevention: Test complex SQL against actual PostgreSQL before shipping. DISTINCT + ORDER BY in aggregates is a known PostgreSQL limitation.

root_cause: OPENAI_STRICT_SCHEMA
details: OpenAI strict JSON schema mode requires additionalProperties: false on ALL nested objects and ALL properties must be in required array. Schema was rejected with 400 Bad Request.
fix: Added additionalProperties: False and complete required arrays to all object definitions in _EXTRACTION_SCHEMA_PASS1
prevention: When building OpenAI JSON schemas with strict=True: every object needs additionalProperties: false and every property in required. Test schema with a real API call before shipping.

root_cause: SEQUENTIAL_IO
details: Two independent DB queries (entity index + vocab context) ran sequentially, adding ~1s to extraction latency unnecessarily.
fix: Wrapped both queries in asyncio.gather() for parallel execution
prevention: When multiple async DB/IO calls are independent, always use asyncio.gather(). Review extraction pipeline for parallelization opportunities.

root_cause: MISSING_SERVICE_WIRING
details: self_awareness.py tried self.deps.get('web_ws_manager') which returned None. WS managers were stored on app.state but never registered in ServiceContainer. ServiceContainer is constructed in buddy_conversation.py and chat_llm.py — neither receives WS managers.
fix: Stored WS managers as module-level refs in state_journal.py via set_ws_managers(), called from main.py Wave 5. Eventually replaced WebSocket round-trip with server-side state collection.
prevention: When a new capability needs a service, verify that service is actually in ServiceContainer before writing self.deps.get() calls. If not, check whether the service is available at construction time and whether wiring it in is justified vs module-level singleton.

root_cause: WRONG_CONN_ID_SCOPE
details: Voice pipeline sets current_conn_id ContextVar to Pipecat's audio transport connection ID. UI state query used this conn_id to call ws_manager.send_to(conn_id), but that ID doesn't exist in WebConnectionManager._clients — it's a different WebSocket connection (Pipecat's transport vs web UI). send_to() returned None/False, interpreted as 'client disconnected'.
fix: Only use conn_id for web_chat channel where it maps to actual web UI connection. For voice pipeline, pass conn_id=None to force broadcast. Eventually replaced with server-side state.
prevention: ContextVars set in the voice pipeline represent Pipecat transport connections, NOT browser UI connections. Never use voice pipeline conn_id to target web UI WebSocket operations.

root_cause: MISSING_INTERFACE_METHODS
details: query_component_state() called ws_manager.has_clients() and checked 'if not sent' on send_to() return. has_clients() didn't exist on either WebConnectionManager or ConnectionManager. send_to() returned None (implicit) not bool. Code was written against an assumed interface the WS managers didn't implement.
fix: Added has_clients() -> bool to both managers. Changed send_to() return type from None to bool.
prevention: When writing code that depends on duck-typed methods, read the actual target class and verify method signatures exist before writing the calling code. No interface/protocol class enforces contracts on these managers.

description: Plan deferred spec requirement to add after_tax_contributions to cashflow_summary, citing issue #155 pattern. Spec v1.3 explicitly requires both locations.
impact: Would violate spec, break frontend summary cards
root_cause: Agent used outdated pattern without checking if spec was updated
lesson: Always validate spec version matches implementation pattern; don't defer explicit spec requirements

description: Plan has contradictory source_id filter logic - Data Structures section requires it, main implementation doesn't enforce it, edge case discussion flip-flops
impact: Undefined behavior - implementer doesn't know which rule to apply
root_cause: Agent identified contradiction but failed to resolve it definitively
lesson: If ambiguity discovered, RESOLVE explicitly; don't leave multiple interpretations

description: Plan computes after_tax_contributions_by_year but never uses it in output conversion
root_cause: Agent copied pattern verbatim without understanding which variables are needed
lesson: Verify every computed variable is used; remove dead code from plan

description: Comment says 'treat None as TAXABLE' but code doesn't implement this behavior
impact: Misleading documentation, confusion for code reviewer
root_cause: Agent wrote defensive comment without checking database constraints
lesson: Only document behavior that's implemented; verify comments match code logic

description: Spec says 'from income source' but plan doesn't clearly enforce source_id filter requirement
impact: Ambiguous implementation - manual contributions might be included incorrectly
root_cause: Agent discussed both interpretations but didn't pick one definitively
lesson: Make definitive decisions on ambiguous requirements; document exclusions clearly

description: Plan adds net_income_after_funding but doesn't verify Net Cash Flow formula needs updating. Spec requires Net Cash Flow = Net Income After Tax - After-Tax Contributions - Expenses - Debt
impact: Breaking change - Net Cash Flow calculation would remain incorrect, overstating by amount of contributions
root_cause: Agent added new field without checking ALL formulas that might need it
lesson: When adding computed field, search for ALL existing formulas that reference related data; verify against spec

description: Plan inserts net_income_after_funding calculation assuming summary['after_tax_contributions'] exists, but doesn't verify ordering. If Net Cash Flow is updated to use after_tax_contributions, it would access column before it's computed (lines 211-215 before 217-228)
impact: KeyError or silent NaN propagation in production
root_cause: Agent didn't verify column dependencies and execution order
lesson: Verify execution order: new calculation must come AFTER all dependencies are available; document dependency chain

description: Plan adds snake_case column 'net_income_after_funding' to DataFrame with existing Title Case columns ('Net Income After Tax'). Inconsistent naming could cause serialization bugs
impact: Potential bugs if code assumes consistent naming convention
root_cause: Agent didn't call out naming inconsistency risk explicitly
lesson: Document column naming conventions and flag inconsistencies; add explicit test for column name format

description: Acceptance criteria says formula uses 'sum(after_tax_contributions[asset][year])' but implementation uses pre-summed 'summary["after_tax_contributions"]'. Dependency on issue #156's totals not explicit
impact: Documentation confusion, dependency assumption not verified
root_cause: Agent copied acceptance criteria from issue description without aligning to implementation approach
lesson: Ensure acceptance criteria match implementation (not just issue description); explicitly document dependencies

description: Plan doesn't specify using pytest.approx() for floating-point assertions, leading to brittle tests that fail spuriously
impact: Test brittleness, spurious failures from floating-point precision errors
root_cause: Agent didn't specify test precision requirements in plan
lesson: Specify pytest.approx() for all floating-point test assertions; default relative tolerance: rel=1e-9

description: Summary card label 'Total Debts' but binds to 'Total Debt Payment' - semantic mismatch between label (stock/balances) and data (flow/payments)
impact: User confusion, misinterpretation of metric meaning
root_cause: Agent didn't validate label semantics match data field semantics
lesson: Validate all UI labels match backend field semantics; 'Debts' implies balances, 'Debt Payment' implies flow

description: Backend computes net_income_after_funding (issue #157) but plan doesn't display it anywhere in 8 summary cards or explain deferral
impact: Wasted backend computation, spec deviation, missing intermediate metric
root_cause: Agent listed field in dependencies but didn't verify it's used in implementation
lesson: Identify all backend fields and verify each is used in implementation; document deferrals explicitly

description: Timeline logic calculates offsets then filters to show only %5===0, would skip ALL years if backend returns sparse data (e.g., offsets [1,6,11,16])
impact: Broken UX - timeline buttons disappear, user can't navigate to valid years
root_cause: Agent assumed consecutive year data, filtered by modulo instead of deriving from actual rows
lesson: Test data access logic with sparse/non-consecutive scenarios; derive UI from actual data, not mathematical filters

description: Projection mode label shows 'Viewing: +{projectionMode} Years' with guard for 'current' but no parsing/validation - would show 'Viewing: +current Years' if guard fails
impact: Incorrect display text in edge cases
root_cause: Agent assumed projectionMode is always numeric string when not 'current', didn't add validation
lesson: Add explicit parsing/validation for display values; don't assume type safety from guards alone

description: Negative income warning only checks 'Net Income After Tax < 0', doesn't warn about negative 'Net Cash Flow' (cash deficit with positive income)
impact: User misses critical cash deficit warnings when income is positive but spending exceeds income
root_cause: Agent only implemented primary warning scenario, didn't consider secondary deficit type
lesson: Consider all warning scenarios for financial metrics; income and cash flow are different deficit types

description: Plan extracts data without validating types - assumes pre_tax_deductions/after_tax_contributions are objects but doesn't validate before calling sumSeries(), runtime error if wrong type
impact: Runtime errors if backend returns unexpected data types (number instead of object)
root_cause: Agent documented expected data shapes but didn't add validation before processing
lesson: Add explicit data type validation before rendering; catch backend schema mismatches early

root_cause: MULTI_MODEL
details: Account model imports Scenario in TYPE_CHECKING block, causing SQLAlchemy mapper initialization failure at runtime
fix: Move Scenario import outside TYPE_CHECKING block in accounts/models.py
prevention: Always import relationship target classes at module level, not in TYPE_CHECKING blocks

root_cause: stub_handlers
